definition_en: the power or skill to do something
example_en: She has the ability to learn quickly.

definition_en: to agree to take or receive something
example_en: He accepted the invitation immediately.

definition_en: a way of entering or using something
example_en: Students have free access to the library.

definition_en: to succeed in doing or reaching something
example_en: They achieved their goal ahead of schedule.

definition_en: from one side to the other side
example_en: She walked across the street.

definition_en: something that you do
example_en: Quick action prevented a serious problem.

definition_en: doing many things; busy and energetic
example_en: She stays active by swimming every day.

definition_en: something that people do for work or enjoyment
example_en: Reading is a relaxing activity for him.

definition_en: in fact; really
example_en: It looked difficult, but it was actually easy.

definition_en: to put something together with something else
example_en: Add a little salt to the soup.

definition_en: details of where a person lives or works
example_en: Please write your address clearly.

definition_en: a fully grown person
example_en: The course is open to all adults.

definition_en: to influence someone or something
example_en: Weather can affect your mood.

definition_en: later than a time or event
example_en: We met after class.

definition_en: one more time
example_en: Could you say that again, please?

definition_en: in opposition to; touching a surface
example_en: The bike was leaning against the wall.

definition_en: the number of years someone has lived
example_en: At the age of ten, she moved abroad.

definition_en: a business or organization that provides a service
example_en: He works for a travel agency.

definition_en: a person who acts for or represents someone else
example_en: Her agent negotiated the contract.

definition_en: used to show how far in the past something happened
example_en: We arrived two days ago.
